advancements.lititup.craft_baled_switchg: Pack 9 Switchgrass fiber into a bale
advancements.lititup.craft_sparkling_fli: Craft sparkling flint by combining flint, stick and switchgrass fiber
advancements.lititup.fire_aspect_ignitio: Ignite a furnace or campfire with Fire Aspect
advancements.lititup.light_furnace.descr: Set a furnace or campfire alight
advancements.lititup.light_furnace.title: Fire, Good
advancements.lititup.rain_ignite.descrip: While it’s raining, manually light a furnace with a heat source beneath it
advancements.lititup.rain_ignite.title: An Inevitable Alternative
advancements.lititup.root.title: Lit It Up
message.lititup.compatibility_descriptio: Some setups add specific ignition options. Differences usually involve augments or fuel requirements. Make sure all required ingredients are inside the furnace before lighting the block.
message.lititup.environmental_ignition_d: A furnace placed above fire, lava or any other custom source will ignite the block as in vanilla. If rain reaches the furnace’s position, this effect is disabled and you must light it manually. Any item with Fire Aspect can also ignite the furnace.
message.lititup.flint_ignition_descripti: By holding a flint in your left hand and another in your right, you can ignite the furnace! Keep flints in both hands and make sure the furnace has switchgrass fiber in the fuel slot. Then right click the block a few times, and there is a small chance it will ignite.
message.lititup.golden_lighter_descripti: Golden Lighter is a rare artifact that lets you light furnaces without holding an ignition tool. It behaves like Flint and Steel with increased durability. When equipped in the Lighter slot (Curios), you can light furnaces while holding any item. Found in Abandoned Mineshafts, Village Toolsmith chests, Buried Treasure, and Desert Pyramids (%s chance).
message.lititup.main_description: To ignite a furnace, first place the required ingredients and fuel inside the block. Then light it with Flint and Steel, Sparkling Flint (requires Switchgrass Fiber in your inventory), or any other available ignition item.
message.lititup.sparkling_flint_descript: Sparkling Flint is an early game alternative to Flint and Steel. Crafted from Flint, a Stick, and Switchgrass Fiber, it removes the need for Iron. It requires Switchgrass Fiber in your inventory to use and has a chance to fail when igniting, but otherwise works like Flint and Steel.
message.lititup.switchgrass_description: Switchgrass grows across the world and is important early on. It drops Switchgrass Fiber, needed to craft Sparkling Flint. Found in most biomes except deserts and hot areas. It spawns 1 to 2 blocks per chunk. Switchgrass has 3 growth stages: at stage 1 it drops nothing, at stage 2 it guarantees 1 Switchgrass Fiber and at full maturity it has a 40% chance to drop an extra fiber. You can also harvest the fully grown block with shears to obtain it as an item.
screen.lititup.title: Lit it Up Guide
